summary: Italian director of Spaghetti Westerns. Best known for the Dollars Trilogy (A Fistful of Dollars, For a Few Dollars More, The Good, the Bad and the Ugly) starring Clint Eastwood, and the later Once Upon a Time in the West. Longtime collaborator with composer Ennio Morricone. A Fistful of Dollars was plagiarized from Kurosawa's Yojimbo, for which Leone was successfully sued. The term 'Ramen Western' (e.g. Tampopo) was coined in homage to his Spaghetti Westerns.

works.name: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)
works.description: The third and final film in the Dollars Trilogy. Tuco ('the Ugly' / Eli Wallach), Angel Eyes ('the Bad' / Lee Van Cleef), and Blondie ('the Good' / Clint Eastwood) seek $200,000 in gold buried in Sad Hill Cemetery. Set during the American Civil War. Score by Ennio Morricone, including 'The Ecstasy of Gold.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Climactic three-way standoff (Mexican standoff) at the Sad Hill Cemetery between Tuco, Angel Eyes, and Blondie; set to Morricone's 'The Ecstasy of Gold'
examples: climaxes in a standoff at the Sad Hill Cemetery between Tuco, Angel Eyes, and Blondie set to Ennio Morricone's 'The Ecstasy of Gold'
This film's soundtrack includes the song 'The Ecstasy of Gold.' A circular open space in the middle of a cemetery is the setting of this film's climactic Mexican standoff

clues.clue: Stars Clint Eastwood (Blondie), Eli Wallach (Tuco), and Lee Van Cleef (Angel Eyes)
examples: featuring Clint Eastwood as one of the three title characters
ends with a three-way showdown between Clint Eastwood, Eli Wallach, and Lee van Cleef

clues.clue: Set during the American Civil War; two armies fight over a bridge
examples: The American Civil War is the setting of The Good, the Bad and the Ugly, including a battle over a bridge

clues.clue: The protagonist is represented by a flute theme; Tuco by an ocarina version of the main theme
examples: The protagonist of this film is represented by a flute version of the main theme, in contrast to the ocarina version representing another character

clues.clue: Tuco says 'When you have to shoot, shoot, don't talk' after killing a man who ambushed him in a bathtub
examples: A character in this film says 'When you have to shoot, shoot, don't talk' after killing a man who had ambushed him while he was in the bathtub

clues.clue: One character earns money by repeatedly claiming bounty on a wanted criminal, then freeing him before he is hanged
examples: One character in this film earns money by repeatedly claiming the reward on a wanted criminal, then freeing him from being hanged

works.name: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)
works.description: A follow-up to the Dollars Trilogy. Henry Fonda cast jarringly as villain Frank. Charles Bronson plays a nameless harmonica-playing protagonist seeking revenge for his brother's death. Claudia Cardinale plays Jill McBain, whose husband Brett dreamed of building the town of Sweetwater. Jason Robards plays bandit Cheyenne. Score by Ennio Morricone. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Henry Fonda cast jarringly as villain Frank; Charles Bronson as nameless harmonica-playing protagonist seeking revenge for his brother
examples: This Leone film jarringly cast Henry Fonda as its villain, Frank. Charles Bronson plays its nameless harmonica-playing protagonist, bent on revenge for the death of his brother

clues.clue: Opens with a 10-minute mostly silent sequence of three killers waiting for the arrival of a train, then shot by the protagonist
examples: In this movie's mostly silent ten minute long opening sequence, three killers wait for the arrival of a train, only to be shot by this movie's protagonist

clues.clue: Land bought by Brett McBain; his dream of building the town of Sweetwater is achieved by wife Jill (Claudia Cardinale)
examples: Its main conflict occurs over land bought by Brett McBain, whose dream of building the town of Sweetwater is achieved by his wife Jill, who is played by Claudia Cardinale

clues.clue: Jason Robards plays bandit Cheyenne; Frank is finally shot by the Harmonica man
examples: Jason Robards plays the bandit Cheyenne in this movie, which ends with Henry Fonda's character Frank being shot by a man only known as 'Harmonica'

clues.clue: A cripple character dies while crawling to a mud puddle and imagining waves of the Pacific Ocean
examples: That character, a cripple, dies while crawling to a mud puddle and imagining the waves of the Pacific Ocean

clues.clue: Martin Scorsese restored Leone's later film Once Upon a Time in America; the 'Once Upon a Time' title structure influenced Tarantino's Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
examples: Leone's later film Once Upon a Time in America was restored by this director [Scorsese]

works.name: Dollars Trilogy (A Fistful of Dollars / For a Few Dollars More)
works.description: Three-film series starring Clint Eastwood as 'The Man with No Name.' A Fistful of Dollars (1964) was plagiarized from Kurosawa's Yojimbo; Leone settled out of court. For a Few Dollars More (1965) was the second entry. The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966) completed the trilogy. Wikipedia

clues.clue: A Fistful of Dollars was plagiarized from Kurosawa's Yojimbo — Leone was sued and settled out of court
examples: This director of The Good, The Bad, and The Ugly remade Yojimbo as the spaghetti western A Fistful of Dollars, after which he was successfully sued by the distributors of Yojimbo for copyright infringement
Sergio Leone settled out of court when his spaghetti Western film A Fistful of Dollars was sued for ripping off the plot of this film [Yojimbo]

clues.clue: Clint Eastwood's 'Man with No Name' appeared in all three films; Eastwood later dedicated Unforgiven to Leone and Don Siegel
examples: This film was dedicated to Sergio Leone and Don Siegel, who had previously cast this film's director in several Westerns
name this actor who played the Man With No Name in Sergio Leone's Dollars trilogy

clues.clue: Dollars Trilogy has three films — the 'three' is a quizbowl clue for the number of entries
examples: For 10 points, name the number of entries in Sergio Leone's Dollars series [answer: three]

works.name: Duck, You Sucker! / A Fistful of Dynamite (1971)
works.description: Leone's Zapata Western. Critiques the leftism of Zapata Westerns. Opens with a close-up shot of bandit Juan urinating on ants. Features an eerie montage of upper-class Mexicans expressing disgust at the poor. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Opens with a shot of bandit Juan urinating on ants; features an eerie montage of upper-class Mexicans expressing disgust at the poor
examples: This Sergio Leone film critiques the leftism of Zapata Westerns and includes an eerie montage of the mouths of upper class Mexicans expressing their disgust at the poor. It begins with a shot of the bandit Juan urinating on some ants

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Biographical and career-level clues about Sergio Leone. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Italian director; pioneer/creator of the Spaghetti Western genre; giveaway identification with The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly
examples: This seminal Spaghetti Western director is best known for his legendary films starring Clint Eastwood, including A Fistful of Dollars and The Good, The Bad, and The Ugly

clues.clue: Longtime collaboration with composer Ennio Morricone — Morricone scored all of Leone's major Westerns
examples: Film composer Ennio Morricone rose to prominence for his soundtracks for films in this genre [Spaghetti Western]
Ennio Morricone composed the scores for The Big Gundown, Django, and The Good, the Bad and the Ugly

clues.clue: 'Ramen Westerns' (e.g. Tampopo) were named in homage to Leone's Spaghetti Westerns
examples: 'Ramen Westerns' like Tampopo are named in homage to this Italian director's Spaghetti Westerns

clues.clue: Leone's films inspired a wave of other 'Once Upon a Time' westerns and neo-westerns (Tarantino, Ceylan, etc.)
examples: Sergio Leone's Once Upon a Time in the West has inspired many spiritual successors [including Tarantino's Once Upon a Time in Hollywood]

clues.clue: Other directors named 'Sergio' in the Spaghetti Western genre: Sergio Corbucci (Django, The Great Silence), Sergio Sollima (The Big Gundown)
examples: Ennio Morricone composed the scores for The Big Gundown, Django, and The Good, the Bad and the Ugly, which were all Spaghetti westerns directed by Italians who share what first name? [Sergio]

comprehensive_summary:
Sergio Leone is an Italian film director and the defining figure of the Spaghetti Western genre. He is most identified with The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966) and its climactic three-way standoff (Mexican standoff) at Sad Hill Cemetery between Tuco, Angel Eyes, and Blondie set to Ennio Morricone's 'The Ecstasy of Gold.' The film stars Clint Eastwood (Blondie), Eli Wallach (Tuco), and Lee Van Cleef (Angel Eyes) against an American Civil War backdrop.

Leone's Dollars Trilogy began with A Fistful of Dollars (1964), which was plagiarized from Akira Kurosawa's Yojimbo — Leone was successfully sued and settled out of court. The trilogy continued with For a Few Dollars More (1965) and concluded with The Good, the Bad and the Ugly. All three starred Clint Eastwood as 'The Man with No Name.' Eastwood later dedicated his own film Unforgiven to Leone and his other mentor Don Siegel.

Once Upon a Time in the West (1968) followed the trilogy. Its casting of Henry Fonda as villain Frank was deliberately jarring. Charles Bronson plays a nameless harmonica-playing protagonist seeking revenge. The film opens with a 10-minute mostly silent sequence of three killers waiting at a train station. Claudia Cardinale plays Jill McBain, whose husband Brett had dreamed of building the town of Sweetwater. Jason Robards plays bandit Cheyenne. Martin Scorsese later restored Leone's final major film, Once Upon a Time in America, and the 'Once Upon a Time' title structure directly inspired Quentin Tarantino's Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.

Leone's Zapata Western Duck, You Sucker! (1971) opens with a close-up of bandit Juan urinating on ants and features an eerie montage of upper-class Mexicans showing disgust at the poor.

Leone's most important collaborator was composer Ennio Morricone, who scored all his major Westerns. The Japanese genre 'Ramen Westerns' (e.g., Itami Juzo's Tampopo) was named as a direct homage to Leone's Spaghetti Westerns. Other notable 'Sergio' directors in the Spaghetti Western genre include Sergio Corbucci (Django, The Great Silence) and Sergio Sollima (The Big Gundown).
